steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures at the blank
steps.content:
Before the blank: “the work of Slovak” (noun phrase).
After the blank: “who aptly describes her photos...” (relative clause).
The blank must be filled with a noun phrase to serve as the antecedent for the relative clause.

steps.title: Apply the rules for appositives and non-restrictive relative clauses
steps.content: When a proper name explicitly identifies a noun, the relative clause following it is non-restrictive and must be preceded by a comma. When a proper noun follows a common noun in apposition, no comma separates them. The correct form is “photographer Mária Švarbová,” — comma after the noun phrase but not between the noun and name. B omits the required comma before “who”; C and D wrongly place commas between “photographer” and the proper name. That matches choice A — A is correct.
